articles.title: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
articles.summary: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

articles.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
articles.summary: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

articles.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
articles.summary: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

articles.title: Row escalates in Ukraine over army chief's future
articles.summary: Protesters are demanding Oleksandr Syrskyi be removed and former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov reinstated by Friday.

articles.title: India's 'cockroach' protest continues a day after police crackdown
articles.summary: Delhi witnessed overwhelming violence on Monday, with at least 60 protesters wounded. Police said 118 of their men were also injured.

articles.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
articles.summary: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

articles.title: Shock and anger after top Ghanaian politician sentenced to 20 years for illegal mining
articles.summary: Bernard Antwi Boasiako, known as "Chairman Wontumi", was also fined more than $10,000 (£7,450).

articles.title: Traffic officers arrested for allegedly seeking bribe from Nigeria's anti-corruption boss
articles.summary: They are accused of stopping Musa Aliyu at a fake roadblock and telling him to drive to a cash machine.

articles.title: At least 27 dead and 83 missing after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana
articles.summary: An investigation into the tragedy has been launched, the island's prime minister says, adding he hopes more will be found alive.

articles.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
articles.summary: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.
